ingredients.item:
During the French colonization in the mid-nineteenth century, people started putting local Vietnamese ingredients on French baguettes and sold them as affordable sandwiches. Bánh mì has remained a popular street food ever since. It’s also consistently ranked as one of the best sandwiches in the world. The basic components include pâté, a main protein (such as egg, cold cuts, grilled meat, or tofu), pickles, fresh herbs, and sauce. However, the fillings are versatile and vary by region. The main proteins for this vegan bánh mì are tofu and seitan flavored with char siu, which is a seasoning mix to make Chinese barbecue pork.
SKILL LEVELAdvanced|YIELD8 servings

VEGAN PÂTÉ
3½ ounces (100 g) dried white beans, soaked in 2 cups (480 ml) water for at least 8 hours (or 10½ ounces/300 g canned white beans, drained, for a quicker version)
1 tablespoon vegetable oil

1 tablespoon minced leek (white part only)
1 tablespoon minced lemongrass
1 Japanese eggplant, cut into ½-inch (1 cm) thick slices
10 raw cashew nuts, soaked in warm water for 30 minutes and peeled
1 tablespoon sugar
2 tablespoons soy sauce
½ teaspoon salt
½ teaspoon unsweetened cocoa powder
Freshly ground black pepper, to taste
¼ cup (60 ml) vegan butter or margarine, melted

FILLING
1 tablespoon minced lemongrass
1 tablespoon minced leek (white part only)

2 tablespoons char siu seasoning mix
1 tablespoon vegetarian oyster sauce
3 tablespoons water
1 tablespoon sugar
½ teaspoon salt
¼ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
1 block (14 ounces/400 g) firm tofu
ingredients.original:
During the French colonization in the mid-nineteenth century, people started putting local Vietnamese ingredients on French baguettes and sold them as affordable sandwiches. Bánh mì has remained a popular street food ever since. It’s also consistently ranked as one of the best sandwiches in the world. The basic components include pâté, a main protein (such as egg, cold cuts, grilled meat, or tofu), pickles, fresh herbs, and sauce. However, the fillings are versatile and vary by region. The main proteins for this vegan bánh mì are tofu and seitan flavored with char siu, which is a seasoning mix to make Chinese barbecue pork.
SKILL LEVELAdvanced|YIELD8 servings

VEGAN PÂTÉ
3½ ounces (100 g) dried white beans, soaked in 2 cups (480 ml) water for at least 8 hours (or 10½ ounces/300 g canned white beans, drained, for a quicker version)
1 tablespoon vegetable oil

1 tablespoon minced leek (white part only)
1 tablespoon minced lemongrass
1 Japanese eggplant, cut into ½-inch (1 cm) thick slices
10 raw cashew nuts, soaked in warm water for 30 minutes and peeled
1 tablespoon sugar
2 tablespoons soy sauce
½ teaspoon salt
½ teaspoon unsweetened cocoa powder
Freshly ground black pepper, to taste
¼ cup (60 ml) vegan butter or margarine, melted

FILLING
1 tablespoon minced lemongrass
1 tablespoon minced leek (white part only)

2 tablespoons char siu seasoning mix
1 tablespoon vegetarian oyster sauce
3 tablespoons water
1 tablespoon sugar
½ teaspoon salt
¼ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
1 block (14 ounces/400 g) firm tofu

ingredients.item: 3½ ounces (100 g) store-bought or homemade seitan
ingredients.original: 3½ ounces (100 g) store-bought or homemade seitan

ingredients.item: 1 medium cucumber, peeled, seeds removed, and cut lengthwise into 3-inch (7.5 cm) slices
ingredients.original: 1 medium cucumber, peeled, seeds removed, and cut lengthwise into 3-inch (7.5 cm) slices

ingredients.item: 3 jalapeño peppers, sliced on the bias (optional)
ingredients.original: 3 jalapeño peppers, sliced on the bias (optional)

ingredients.item: 1. MAKE THE VEGAN PÂTÉ If using canned beans, skip to step 2. Place dried beans in a medium saucepan and fill with enough water to cover. Bring to a boil over high heat, and then cover and simmer on low heat until beans are tender but not mushy (20 to 30 minutes). Remove from heat and drain.
ingredients.original: 1. MAKE THE VEGAN PÂTÉ If using canned beans, skip to step 2. Place dried beans in a medium saucepan and fill with enough water to cover. Bring to a boil over high heat, and then cover and simmer on low heat until beans are tender but not mushy (20 to 30 minutes). Remove from heat and drain.

ingredients.item: 2. In a medium skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of oil over medium-low heat. When hot, add leek and lemongrass, and cook and stir until fragrant (about 2 minutes). Add Japanese eggplant and cashews, and cook and stir until slightly browned and softened (3 to 5 minutes). Add precooked or canned white beans, sugar, soy sauce, and salt. Cook and stir for 2 minutes, and then turn off heat and let cool.
ingredients.original: 2. In a medium skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of oil over medium-low heat. When hot, add leek and lemongrass, and cook and stir until fragrant (about 2 minutes). Add Japanese eggplant and cashews, and cook and stir until slightly browned and softened (3 to 5 minutes). Add precooked or canned white beans, sugar, soy sauce, and salt. Cook and stir for 2 minutes, and then turn off heat and let cool.

ingredients.item: 3. Transfer cooled mixture to a blender or food processor, add cocoa powder, and process until smooth.
ingredients.original: 3. Transfer cooled mixture to a blender or food processor, add cocoa powder, and process until smooth.

ingredients.item: 4. Pour the purée into a 2-cup (480 ml) container, sprinkle with ground pepper, and drizzle melted margarine on top. Cover and refrigerate for 4 hours.
ingredients.original: 4. Pour the purée into a 2-cup (480 ml) container, sprinkle with ground pepper, and drizzle melted margarine on top. Cover and refrigerate for 4 hours.

ingredients.item: 5. MAKE THE FILLING In a large bowl, combine lemongrass, leek, char siu seasoning mix, vegetarian oyster sauce, water, sugar, salt, and ¼ teaspoon of ground pepper, and stir well. Cut tofu into 1-inch (2.5 cm) thick steaks and add to bowl along with seitan. Toss well to coat with sauce, and let marinate for 30 minutes.
ingredients.original: 5. MAKE THE FILLING In a large bowl, combine lemongrass, leek, char siu seasoning mix, vegetarian oyster sauce, water, sugar, salt, and ¼ teaspoon of ground pepper, and stir well. Cut tofu into 1-inch (2.5 cm) thick steaks and add to bowl along with seitan. Toss well to coat with sauce, and let marinate for 30 minutes.

ingredients.item: 6. Scrape off the minced aromatics and shake off excess marinade from the tofu and seitan, but leave remaining marinade in the bowl.
ingredients.original: 6. Scrape off the minced aromatics and shake off excess marinade from the tofu and seitan, but leave remaining marinade in the bowl.

ingredients.item: 7. In a medium skillet, heat ¼ cup (60 ml) of oil over medium heat. When hot, add tofu and seitan, and cook until golden brown on all sides (about 10 minutes). Remove from heat, let cool, and cut both into slices.
ingredients.original: 7. In a medium skillet, heat ¼ cup (60 ml) of oil over medium heat. When hot, add tofu and seitan, and cook until golden brown on all sides (about 10 minutes). Remove from heat, let cool, and cut both into slices.

ingredients.item: 8. Put reserved marinade in a small saucepan and simmer over low heat until slightly thickened (2 to 3 minutes). Strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a small bowl and set aside.
ingredients.original: 8. Put reserved marinade in a small saucepan and simmer over low heat until slightly thickened (2 to 3 minutes). Strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a small bowl and set aside.

ingredients.item: 9. ASSEMBLE Cut open each baguette lengthwise. Spread a thin layer of pâté on one side, and then drizzle with sauce. Add pickled carrots and daikon, cilantro, cucumber, tofu, seitan, and jalapeño pepper slices (if using), and serve immediately.
ingredients.original: 9. ASSEMBLE Cut open each baguette lengthwise. Spread a thin layer of pâté on one side, and then drizzle with sauce. Add pickled carrots and daikon, cilantro, cucumber, tofu, seitan, and jalapeño pepper slices (if using), and serve immediately.
